strengths: Explicit truth-maintenance mechanisms (validity intervals, contradict/supersede edges) address a critical gap in long-horizon agent memory
Single-session-user slice shows strict set-inclusion improvement over Flat RAG (100% vs 90.5%)
Abstention policy is behaviorally distinct and verifiable via explicit flag, not downstream hallucination
Efficiency gains over A-Mem (-62.1% tokens) demonstrate practical deployability
weaknesses: Overall accuracy statistically indistinguishable from baseline despite targeted improvements
Paired tests on capability slices (SSU, ABS) underpowered due to small n (21 and 9 questions)
Structural limitations in handling assistant-authored content (F1), preference reasoning (F2), and multi-session aggregation (F3)
Intent classifier for TTMG+R-LLM has low recall on multi-session (42.5%) and knowledge-update (39.1%) slices
required_revisions: Improve assistant-side claim extraction to address F1 failures
Add multi-claim composition in reader prompt to handle F3-style aggregation
Replace zero-shot Kimi-K2 intent classifier with a fine-tuned model to boost router recall
Clarify that non-significant overall accuracy (p=0.21) reflects complementary strengths, not inferiority

strengths: Novel claim-level memory schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Explicit abstention mechanism with 80% abstention rate on full abstention slice
Ablation confirms LLM linker's causal role in all measured gains
Efficiency gains over A-Mem (-62.1% tokens, -27.4% latency)
weaknesses: Overall accuracy not statistically distinguishable from Flat RAG (p=0.21)
Low power in SSU (n=21) and abstention (n=9) slices (McNemar p≥0.0625)
Structural regressions on assistant-authored (SSA), preference (SSP), and multi-session (MS) slices
Zero-shot intent classifier has low recall on MS (42.5%) and KU (39.1%) slices
required_revisions: Increase sample size for SSU and abstention slices to achieve conventional statistical power
Address structural failure modes (F1-F3) via denser assistant-side claim extraction and multi-claim composition
Improve intent classifier recall on MS and KU slices to close oracle gap
Clarify how session-batched writing resolves cross-session coreference for MS questions

strengths: Explicit truth-maintenance edges (contradict/supersede) enable precise temporal reasoning and abstention policies
Session-batched writer reduces LLM call overhead compared to per-turn analysis
Greedy maximum-consistent-subgraph algorithm provides efficient deterministic retrieval with near-optimal guarantees
Hybrid reader context preserves flat retrieval's strengths while adding structured truth filtering
Ablation isolates the LLM linker as the causal mechanism for gains on targeted slices
weaknesses: Overall accuracy statistically indistinguishable from Flat RAG despite structured claims
Explicit abstention policy only behaviorally different, not accuracy-improved, on full abstention slice
Regression on assistant-authored, preference, and multi-session questions not fully addressed
Intent classifier for TTMG+R-LLM has low recall on multi-session (42.5%) and knowledge-update (39.1%) slices
Bootstrap CIs for TTMG overall accuracy [53.3,69.3] overlap with Flat RAG [60.7,75.3]
required_revisions: Clarify how the session-batched writer resolves cross-turn coreference before claim extraction
Quantify the cost of the Kimi-K2 zero-shot intent classifier in the TTMG+R-LLM router
Explain why the TTMG-Abstain router's abstention behavior doesn't propagate to higher accuracy on the abstention slice
Provide more details on the temporal parser's handling of implicit time anchors like 'last Friday'
Justify the choice of 3 LLM models (Kimi-K2 for writer/linker/parser vs DeepSeek for reader)

strengths: Novel claim-level memory schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges (contradict/supersede)
Rigorous empirical protocol: paired McNemar tests, bootstrap CIs, stratified sampling, ablation study
Clear efficiency win: −62 % tokens vs A-Mem with lower latency
Open-source release of prompts, code, and per-question outputs ensures reproducibility
Ablation cleanly isolates the LLM linker as the causal driver of gains on abstention & knowledge-update
weaknesses: Overall accuracy statistically ties Flat RAG (61.3 % vs 68.0 %, p=0.21) while regressing on three slices (SSA, SSP, MS)
Small-slice paired wins (ABS n=9, SSU n=21) are under-powered (McNemar p≥0.06) and do not generalise to the full 30-question abstention set
Intent classifier recall on multi-session is only 42.5 %, leaving a 12–15 pp gap to the oracle upper bound
Writer under-extracts assistant-authored claims (F1 failure mode), causing over-abstention
Evaluation limited to 150-question subset; authors defer full 500-question replication to camera-ready
required_revisions: Run and report the full LongMemEval-S 500-question evaluation to remove subset-representativeness concerns
Provide power analysis or increase sample size for the small slices (ABS, SSU) to turn directional trends into significant wins
Ablate or improve the intent classifier to close the 12–15 pp gap between deployable routers and the oracle
Address writer gap for assistant-authored turns (F1) via denser extraction or joint user/assistant claim generation
Clarify why overall accuracy drops despite slice-level wins—discuss error propagation and trade-offs explicitly

strengths: Novel claim-level schema with validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Explicit abstention mechanism with 80% trigger rate vs 0% for flat RAG
Thorough ablation showing LLM linker is causal for all measured gains
Efficiency win: -62% tokens vs A-Mem with lower latency
Full reproducibility package: prompts, seeds, per-question outputs, scripts
weaknesses: Under-powered slices: n=9 abstention & n=21 SSU lack statistical significance (p>0.05)
No error bars or variance on per-slice numbers
Only 150-question subset evaluated for TTMG; 500-question run promised for camera-ready
Router variants lose TTMG abstention benefit on ABS questions by construction
Intent classifier recall on MS/KU only 42%/39%, limiting router ceiling
required_revisions: Run full 500-question LongMemEval-S for TTMG and report 95% CIs on every slice
Provide exact counts (b,c) and two-sided McNemar p-values for all paired comparisons
Add bootstrap or jackknife variance estimates for per-slice accuracies
Include a significance test for the –contradict ablation drops (-2.6pp overall, -22pp ABS)
Clarify why routers default to Flat RAG on ABS instead of preserving TTMG abstention

strengths: Claim-level schema with validity intervals and explicit contradict/supersede edges is a clean, practical abstraction for truth maintenance
Session-batched writer + lexical gate keeps write-time cost low (62 % token reduction vs A-Mem)
Ablation shows LLM linker is causal: -22 pp abstention, -9 pp knowledge-update when removed
Router composition recovers Flat-RAG overall accuracy while preserving abstention behaviour
Full reproducibility: code, prompts, seeds, per-question outputs released
weaknesses: Overall accuracy statistically ties Flat RAG (61.3 % vs 68.0 %, p=0.21) so headline gain is narrow
Intent classifier recall low on multi-session (42.5 %) and knowledge-update (39.1 %) slices
Evaluation powered only for large deltas; SSU n=21 and ABS n=9 trends are under-powered
Writer under-extracts assistant-authored claims (7/7 SSA errors) – systematic coverage gap
No cross-encoder reranker or latency breakdown; 85 s E2E latency may still be too high for chat
Only one benchmark (LongMemEval-S); no test on real multi-week deployment or user study
required_revisions: Run the promised N=500 full-set TTMG replication and report exact McNemar p-value
Add latency breakdown: writer, linker, parser, reader per question; quantify linker hit-rate
Provide confidence intervals for per-slice accuracies (currently only overall CI given)
Include a micro-benchmark of write-time throughput: claims/second vs conversation turns/second
Discuss failure-mode mitigation: denser assistant extraction, multi-claim reader composition
Clarify cost model: token price per 1k questions and CPU/memory overhead vs baseline RAG

strengths: Novel problem framing: truth maintenance for long-horizon agent memory is under-studied and well-motivated
Clean methodological contribution: claim-level schema with validity intervals, provenance, and explicit contradict/supersede edges
Thorough ablation study demonstrating causal role of LLM linker across all measured slices
Honest reporting of regressions, underpowered tests, and detailed failure mode taxonomy (F1-F3)
Meaningful efficiency gains: 62% token reduction vs A-Mem baseline with session-batched writing
Practical router composition approach for deployment (TTMG-Abstain, TTMG+R-LLM)
weaknesses: Overall accuracy numerically regresses (61.3% vs 68.0%) even if not statistically significant (p=0.21)
Key capability claims rely on underpowered tests: SSU n=21 (p=0.50), abstention n=9 (p=0.125)
Full abstention set (n=30) shows no accuracy difference between TTMG and Flat RAG (both 76.7%)
Main evaluation on 150/500 question subset; N=500 TTMG results deferred to camera-ready
A-Mem baseline row in Table 1 has missing values (--), incomplete comparison
Router compositions do not preserve abstention benefit (ABS accuracy equals Flat's 44.4%)
Intent classifier weak (64.7% overall), limiting router effectiveness
No comparison to other structured memory baselines (MemGPT, MemoryBank, MemoryAgentBench)
required_revisions: Complete N=500 evaluation for TTMG to match the full LongMemEval-S scale verification done for Flat RAG
Fill missing A-Mem values in Table 1 (SSA, SSP, SSU columns) or remove the row entirely
Increase sample size for abstention and SSU slices to achieve adequate statistical power (current n=9, n=21 are underpowered)
Add comparison to at least one additional structured memory baseline beyond A-Mem (e.g., MemGPT)
Clarify why explicit abstention behavior (80% vs 0%) does not translate to accuracy gains on full n=30 abstention set
Report per-question latency breakdown (writer/linker/reader) rather than only end-to-end
Address or acknowledge that router compositions currently discard TTMG's primary abstention advantage

strengths: Novel application of truth maintenance concepts to LLM agent memory with claim-level schema and contradict/supersede edges
Thorough failure-mode analysis categorizing all 18 paired errors into three structural causes (F1-F3)
Honest reporting of statistical power limitations and underpowered tests on key slices
Good reproducibility provisions: full prompts, hyper-parameters, seed, and promise to release code and raw outputs
Meaningful efficiency gains: -62.1% tokens vs A-Mem baseline
Router composition analysis demonstrates complementary information between claim-level and raw-text retrieval
weaknesses: Main improvement claims (SSU 2-0 paired, abstention 4-0) are NOT statistically significant (p=0.50 and p=0.125); paper overstates these as 'wins'
Overall accuracy regresses from 68.0% to 61.3% (though not significant, p=0.21); framing as 'statistical parity' obscures negative direction
A-Mem baseline is self-reimplemented rather than using original; shows incomplete evaluation (SSA/SSP columns are '--') and no confidence intervals
Limited ablation coverage: only linker removal tested; should ablate claim schema, temporal parser, max-consistent-subgraph retriever, and subject-predicate gate
TTMG not evaluated on full N=500 benchmark; only N=150 stratified subset due to compute cost, limiting generalizability
Router compositions (67.3%, 66.0%) numerically below Flat RAG (68.0%) despite claiming 'matching' accuracy
required_revisions: Report effect sizes with confidence intervals for all paired comparisons, not just p-values
Use original A-Mem implementation or validate reimplementation against published benchmarks
Complete A-Mem evaluation on all slices with confidence intervals
Add ablations for: (a) claim schema vs raw notes, (b) temporal parser, (c) max-consistent-subgraph retriever
Run TTMG on full N=500 LongMemEval-S to verify subset generalizability
Tone down claims of 'strict set-inclusion improvement' and 'paired wins' when tests are underpowered and non-significant
Report standard errors or CIs for all per-slice accuracies, not just overall

strengths: Real efficiency gains: -62.1% token cost vs A-Mem (13603 vs 35904 tokens/question)
Practical integration surface via inference-time routers (TTMG-Abstain, TTMG+R-LLM)
Explicit abstention mechanism (80% on abstention questions) provides safety benefit
Well-designed ablation showing causal role of LLM linker across all slices
Honest reporting of regressions, statistical limitations, and failure mode taxonomy
Full reproducibility: prompts, hyperparameters, scripts, and per-question outputs
weaknesses: Overall accuracy numerically lower than Flat RAG (61.3% vs 68.0%), though not statistically significant (p=0.21)
Main evaluation on 150-question subset only, not full 500-question benchmark
Regressions on SSA (-35.3pp), SSP (-33.4pp), MS (-7.5pp) slices not addressed in method
Intent classifier weak (64.7% overall, 42.5% recall on multi-session) limits router effectiveness
Key slice results underpowered (SSU n=21, McNemar p=0.50; ABS n=9 in subset)
Oracle gap (82% vs 67%) shows significant unrealized potential from claim/text complementarity
required_revisions: Run full 500-question LongMemEval-S evaluation for TTMG to confirm subset representativeness
Address SSA/SSP/MS regressions: denser assistant-side extraction, multi-claim reader composition
Report per-slice confidence intervals in addition to overall CI
Add comparison of memory footprint and index size between TTMG, Flat RAG, and A-Mem
Clarify deployment recipe: which router variant is recommended and under what conditions
